channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer physical edition to be distributed by PLAION
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

High speed races across the Star Wars galaxy.

PLAION has signed an agreement with publisher Secret Mode to act as the global physical distributor of the Fuse Games-developed racing game Star Wars: Galactic Racer.

Under the agreement, PLAION will manage all aspects of the physical edition’s logistics, distribution, and retail sales operations across all key international territories.

“We are delighted to partner with Secret Mode to bring Star Wars: Galactic Racer to the global physical market,” said PLAION Partners CEO Craig McNicol in a press release. “Securing the mandate for worldwide physical distribution of such an exciting title underscores the strength of our retail relationships, our expert teams around the world and our extensive experience within the physical games space. We are committed to delivering top-tier products to players everywhere and look forward to a strong global launch.”

Secret Mode publishing commercial director Alison Fraser added, “We at Secret Mode are delighted to announce Plaion as our trusted physical distribution partner to help deliver global success on our recently announced game, Star Wars: Galactic Racer. We have full confidence in their experience and expertise and look forward to working together to bring this exhilarating racing game to both new and long-standing fans of the beloved franchise.”

Star Wars: Galactic Racer is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store in 2026.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/star-wars-galactic-racer-physical-edition-to-be-distributed-by-plaion
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Blue Reflection 9th anniversary live stream set for March 27
item.content:encoded:
Look back on the series with Mel Kishida and voice actors.

Publisher Koei Tecmo and developer Gust will host the Blue Reflection 9th Anniversary Live Stream on March 27 at 20:00 JST, the companies announced. You will be able to watch it on YouTube, Twitter, and Niconico.

The broadcast will look back on the Blue Reflection series as it approaches its ninth anniversary on March 30. Program guests include series character designer and supervisor Mel Kishida, Hinako Shirai voice actor Yuuki Takada, and Raimu Shijou voice actor Sawako Hata.

The Blue Reflection series consists of the 2017-released Blue Reflection for PlayStation 4, PS Vita, and PC (Steam), the 2021-released Blue Reflection: Second Light for PlayStation 4, Switch, and PC (Steam), and the 2023-released Blue Reflection Sun for iOS, and Android, as well as the 2021 anime Blue Reflection Ray.

Watch a teaser video below.

Teaser



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/blue-reflection-9th-anniversary-live-stream-set-for-march-27
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem sales top six million
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

One million units sold since March 4.

Total sales for Resident Evil Requiem have surpassed six million units, Capcom announced.

Resident Evil Requiem launched for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on February 27.

Sales were last reported to have surpassed five million units on March 4.

Here is an overview of the game, via Capcom:

Resident Evil Requiem is the latest installment in the Resident Evil series, which offers photorealistic visuals and a deep sense of immersion. Players can enjoy the elevated essence of the survival horror experience by the interplay between intense fear and exhilarating action, made possible by two protagonists. Going forward, Capcom plans to implement several measures, such as ongoing support and additional game content, so players can continue to enjoy the title longer.

In addition, the Resident Evil series will celebrate its 30th anniversary on March 22, 2026. Capcom is readying various plans for this anniversary to delight series fans, such as a collaboration between Universal Studios Japan and Resident Evil Requiem in 2026, and orchestral concerts in Japan, the United States, and Europe.

Capcom remains firmly committed to satisfying the expectations of all users by leveraging its industry leading game development capabilities in order to create highly entertaining gameplay experiences.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/resident-evil-requiem-sales-top-six-million
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
